maintenanceScenario: A maintainer changes empty Todo search to return no results because it appears simpler.
vibeCodingRisk: Without the original UX intent, an AI-assisted maintenance pass may optimize the search implementation and accidentally remove the clear-search full-list behavior.

intentRecords.statement: When the Todo search query is empty, the UI must show the full list to preserve browsing context after clearing search.
intentRecords.maintenanceRiskIfMissing: The empty-query branch may be rewritten to show no results, making clear-search feel like data loss.
intentRecords.preservationRule: Do not change empty-query behavior unless Product acceptance explicitly changes.
intentRecords.nonGoal: Do not treat empty search as a filter that intentionally hides all todos.
intentRecords.fallbackReason: If graph-source projection is unavailable, the Product Tree acceptance criterion remains the fallback/reference for this intent.
intentRecords.evidenceReason: A runtime or validation check should prove that clearing the query restores the full list.
intentRecords.compatibilityReason: Task-card or read-model views may summarize this behavior, but they must not weaken the acceptance intent.

edgeIntent.claim: empty search restores the full list after clearing a query

readModelIntentFields.projectionBoundary: Read-model projections must carry intent-critical maintenance notes as Evidence/context, not as user acceptance replacement.

boundaries.sourceAuthorityBoundary: This fixture demonstrates native intent capture only; it does not change repo-wide source authority.
boundaries.nonPromotionStatement: This fixture is not Graph-source promotion, enforcement, required-check configuration, or tree retirement.
boundaries.userAcceptanceBoundary: Only the user can approve changing the recorded intent.
